speaker: TALBOT
text: My gracious prince and honorable peers,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Hearing of your arrival in this realm,

speaker: TALBOT
text: I have awhile given truce unto my wars

speaker: TALBOT
text: To do my duty to my sovereign;

speaker: TALBOT
text: In sign whereof, this arm, that hath reclaimed

speaker: TALBOT
text: To your obedience fifty fortresses,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Twelve cities, and seven walled towns of strength,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Besides five hundred prisoners of esteem,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Lets fall his sword before your Highness’ feet,

speaker: TALBOT
text: And with submissive loyalty of heart

speaker: TALBOT
text: Ascribes the glory of his conquest got

speaker: TALBOT
text: First to my God, and next unto your Grace.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Is this the Lord Talbot, Uncle Gloucester,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That hath so long been resident in France?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Yes, if it please your Majesty, my liege.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Welcome, brave captain and victorious lord.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: When I was young—as yet I am not old—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I do remember how my father said

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: A stouter champion never handled sword.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Long since we were resolvèd of your truth,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Your faithful service, and your toil in war;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Yet never have you tasted our reward

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Or been reguerdoned with so much as thanks,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Because till now we never saw your face.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Therefore stand up; and for these good deserts

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: We here create you Earl of Shrewsbury;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And in our coronation take your place.

speaker: VERNON
text: Now, sir, to you that were so hot at sea,

speaker: VERNON
text: Disgracing of these colors that I wear

speaker: VERNON
text: In honor of my noble Lord of York,

speaker: VERNON
text: Dar’st thou maintain the former words thou spak’st?

speaker: BASSET
text: Yes, sir, as well as you dare patronage

speaker: BASSET
text: The envious barking of your saucy tongue

speaker: BASSET
text: Against my lord the Duke of Somerset.

speaker: VERNON
text: Sirrah, thy lord I honor as he is.

speaker: BASSET
text: Why, what is he? As good a man as York.

speaker: VERNON
text: Hark you, not so; in witness, take you that.

speaker: BASSET
text: Villain, thou knowest the law of arms is such

speaker: BASSET
text: That whoso draws a sword ’tis present death,

speaker: BASSET
text: Or else this blow should broach thy dearest blood.

speaker: BASSET
text: But I’ll unto his Majesty, and crave

speaker: BASSET
text: I may have liberty to venge this wrong,

speaker: BASSET
text: When thou shalt see I’ll meet thee to thy cost.

speaker: VERNON
text: Well, miscreant, I’ll be there as soon as you,

speaker: VERNON
text: And after meet you sooner than you would.
